title: Bridge Essay: A Systematic Review of Aphantasia: Concept, Measurement, Neural Basis, and Theor

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through A, Systematic, Review, of, Aphantasia:.

**A Systematic Review of Aphantasia: Concept, Measurement, Neural Basis, and Theory Development.** — Vision (Basel). PMID: 39330760. DOI: 10.3390/vision8030056. People with aphantasia exhibit the inability to voluntarily generate or form mental imagery in their minds. Since the term "aphantasia" was proposed to describe this, it has gained increasing attention from psychiatrists, neuroscientists, and clinicians. Previous studies have mainly focused on the definition, prevalence, and measurement of aphantasia, its impacts on individuals' cognitive and emotional processing, and theoretical frameworks synthesizing existing findings, which have contributed
